<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Commutative algebra</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}

  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

← Notebook
1 Ideals & Spec 2 Prime vs maximal 3 Zariski topology 4 Radicals 5 Modules & tensor 6 Noetherian 7 Artinian local rings 8 Localization 9 Flatness 10 Nakayama 11 Integral extensions 12 Dimension 13 Transcendence degree

Commutative algebra, from primes to dimension

Rings, their ideals, and the geometry hiding inside them. A working tour through the six things you actually use: spectra, modules, localization, flatness, Nakayama, and Krull dimension.

blocks.html:
1. Ideals, primes, and the spectrum

Every ring in sight is commutative with $1$. A prime ideal $\mathfrak{p}\subset A$ is one where $A/\mathfrak{p}$ is an integral domain; maximal if $A/\mathfrak{p}$ is a field. The spectrum

$\mathrm{Spec}(A) = \{\mathfrak{p}\subset A : \mathfrak{p}\text{ prime}\}$

is a topological space with closed sets $V(I)=\{\mathfrak{p}:I\subseteq\mathfrak{p}\}$. Two pictures to keep side by side:

arithmetic $\mathrm{Spec}(\mathbb{Z})$ is the primes $(2),(3),(5),\dots$ plus a generic point $(0)$. Each closed point is a "residue $\mathbb{F}_p$".

geometric $\mathrm{Spec}(k[x,y])$ has closed points $(x-a,y-b)$ (the actual points of $\mathbb{A}^2_k$), height-1 primes $(f)$ for irreducible $f$ (curves), and the generic point $(0)$.

Ideals are the fundamental subobjects. Below, the Hasse diagram of ideals for small rings. Primes are the ones sitting "below" chain tops in a very particular sense — geometrically, closed irreducible subsets.

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 1: ideal lattice =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#lat-svg'), pick = $('#lat-ring'), info = $('#lat-info');

  /* build lattice data from ring spec */
  function buildZn(n){
    const divs = divisors(n);
    // ideal (d) corresponds to d | n; (d)=(e) iff d=e up to unit, here d is the generator
    // lattice: (d1) subseteq (d2) iff d2 | d1
    const primes = new Set(factorizeInt(n).map(([p])=>p));
    const nodes = divs.map(d=>{
      const quot = n/d; // |R/I| = d? No: ideal (d) in Z/n has size n/d so |R/(d)| = d
      // actually: (d) as subset of Z/n equals dZ/nZ, which has n/d elements; quotient has d elements
      const quotSize = d;
      // Is (d) prime in Z/n? Iff Z/n / (d) = Z/d is a domain, iff d is prime or d=1? d=1 gives (1)=R, not prime.
      // Actually quotient Z/n /(d) as a group is Z/d if d | n. Is it a domain? It's Z/d, which is a domain iff d=0 or d prime. d ranges in divisors of n, so d>=1.
      // So prime iff d is prime. Maximal iff Z/d is a field iff d prime. So prime=maximal here (Artinian).
      const isPrime = primes.has(d);
      const isMaximal = isPrime;
      // primary: (d) primary iff quotient has only one associated prime; in Z/n, (d) primary iff d is a prime power
      const isPrimePower = (d>1) && factorizeInt(d).length===1;
      const isPrimary = isPrimePower;
      // radical of (d) in Z/n: rad = product of distinct primes dividing d (intersected with the primes of n)
      let rad = 1;
      for(const [p] of factorizeInt(d)) rad *= p;
      // but rad in Z/n means smallest d' | n with same prime support as d:
      let radGen = rad;
      // if d=1 -> rad = n (the full ideal? No, rad of (1) in Z/n... (1)=unit ideal so rad=(1))
      if(d===1) radGen = 1;
      else {
        // adjust: radGen should divide n
        while(n%radGen!==0) radGen++;
      }
      const isRadical = (d===radGen);
      return {
        id: `d${d}`,
        label: d===1 ? '(1)=R' : (d===n ? `(${d})=(0)` : `(${d})`),
        gen: d,
        quotSize,
        isPrime, isMaximal, isPrimary, isRadical,
        info: (()=>{
          const lines = [];
          lines.push(`ideal (${d}) in ℤ/${n}`);
          lines.push(`quotient R/I ≅ ℤ/${d}`);
          lines.push(`|R/I| = ${d}`);
          if(d===1) lines.push('this is the unit ideal');
          if(d===n) lines.push('this is the zero ideal');
          const tags = [];
          if(isMaximal) tags.push('maximal');
          else if(isPrime) tags.push('prime');
          if(isPrimary && !isPrime) tags.push('primary');
          if(isRadical) tags.push('radical');
          if(tags.length) lines.push('type: ' + tags.join(', '));
          return lines.join('\n');
        })(),
      };
    });
    // edges: (d1) directly contains (d2) means d2 is a multiple of d1 and d2/d1 is prime
    const edges = [];
    for(const a of nodes) for(const b of nodes){
      if(a.gen!==b.gen && b.gen%a.gen===0){
        const q = b.gen/a.gen;
        if(isPrime(q)){
          // a ⊇ b (bigger ideal contains smaller)
          edges.push([b.id, a.id]); // draw from smaller (lower) to bigger (upper)
        }
      }
    }
    return { nodes, edges, title:`ℤ/${n}ℤ`, orderBy:'gen' };
  }

  function buildPolyQuot(p, f, fDisplay){
    // ring R = F_p[x] / (f). Ideals correspond to monic divisors g | f.
    // enumerate all monic divisors of f by trying all monic polys of degree 0..deg f
    const d = f.length-1;
    if(d>4){ return null; }
    // enumerate all monic polys of degree 1..d, and check divides f
    const divs = [];
    // divisor (1) i.e. the unit ideal: generator 1
    divs.push([1]);
    for(let dg=1; dg<=d; dg++){
      // iterate over leading-coeff-1 polys of degree dg
      const coefs = Array(dg).fill(0);
      function rec(i){
        if(i===dg){
          const g = coefs.concat([1]); // leading 1
          const {q,r} = polyDivMod(f, g, p);
          if(polyIsZero(r)){
            divs.push(polyTrim(g));
          }
          return;
        }
        for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[i]=c; rec(i+1); }
      }
      rec(0);
    }
    // dedupe
    const seen = new Set();
    const divs2 = [];
    for(const g of divs){
      const k = g.join(',');
      if(!seen.has(k)){ seen.add(k); divs2.push(g); }
    }
    // For each divisor g, the ideal (g) in R satisfies (g) ⊇ (h) iff g | h
    // primes of R are (g) for g monic irreducible factor of f
    // need to factor f into irreducibles to identify primes
    // factorization: try all monic polys of degree 1..d/2 and see if divides
    function factorIrreducible(a){
      // returns list of [irred factor, multiplicity]
      const out = [];
      const da = a.length-1;
      let cur = a.slice();
      for(let dg=1; dg<=da; dg++){
        // iterate monics of degree dg
        let found = true;
        while(found){
          found = false;
          const coefs = Array(dg).fill(0);
          let chosen = null;
          function rec(i){
            if(chosen) return;
            if(i===dg){
              const g = coefs.concat([1]);
              const {q,r} = polyDivMod(cur, g, p);
              if(polyIsZero(r) && g.length-1===dg){
                // check g is irreducible: no divisor of degree 1..dg/2
                let irr = true;
                if(dg>1){
                  for(let dgg=1; dgg<=dg/2 && irr; dgg++){
                    const coefs2 = Array(dgg).fill(0);
                    function rec2(j){
                      if(!irr) return;
                      if(j===dgg){
                        const h = coefs2.concat([1]);
                        const {r:rr2} = polyDivMod(g, h, p);
                        if(polyIsZero(rr2)) irr = false;
                        return;
                      }
                      for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs2[j]=c; rec2(j+1); }
                    }
                    rec2(0);
                  }
                }
                if(irr) chosen = g;
              }
              return;
            }
            for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[i]=c; rec(i+1); }
          }
          rec(0);
          if(chosen){
            const {q} = polyDivMod(cur, chosen, p);
            cur = q;
            // add to out
            const existing = out.find(e=>polyEq(e[0], chosen));
            if(existing) existing[1]++;
            else out.push([chosen, 1]);
            found = true;
            if(cur.length-1 < dg) break;
          }
        }
      }
      return out;
    }
    const fFact = factorIrreducible(f);

    function isPrimeIdeal(g){
      // (g) prime iff g is irreducible (in R = F_p[x]/(f), ideal (g) where g | f)
      // (g) ≠ R requires g != unit
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return false;
      if(g.length-1===0) return false;
      // g is irreducible iff no divisor of degree 1..deg(g)/2
      const dg = g.length-1;
      for(let dgg=1; dgg<=dg/2; dgg++){
        const coefs = Array(dgg).fill(0);
        let flag = false;
        function rec(j){
          if(flag) return;
          if(j===dgg){
            const h = coefs.concat([1]);
            const {r:rr} = polyDivMod(g, h, p);
            if(polyIsZero(rr) && g.length-1 > dgg) flag = true;
            return;
          }
          for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[j]=c; rec(j+1); }
        }
        rec(0);
        if(flag) return false;
      }
      return true;
    }

    function isPrimaryIdeal(g){
      // in R = F_p[x]/(f), (g) primary iff g is a power of an irreducible
      // meaning g = q^k for some irreducible q
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return false;
      if(g.length-1===0) return false;
      // factor g
      const gf = factorIrreducible(g);
      return gf.length===1;
    }

    function radicalOf(g){
      // radical in R: product of distinct irreducible factors, intersected with structure.
      // Here rad((g)) = (square-free part of g, reduced mod f)
      // But in R = F_p[x]/(f), we only consider g | f, so factors are among factors of f.
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return [1];
      const gf = factorIrreducible(g);
      let r = [1];
      for(const [q] of gf) r = polyMul(r, q, p);
      // reduce mod f just in case (though r | f)
      return polyMod(r, f, p);
    }

    const nodes = divs2.map(g=>{
      const isPr = isPrimeIdeal(g);
      // maximal iff quotient is a field; R/(g) as F_p[x]/(g)... but R = F_p[x]/(f), so R/(g) = F_p[x]/(g) when g|f
      // R/(g) field iff g irreducible. So maximal iff prime (true in this Artinian quotient).
      const isMax = isPr;
      const isPrimary = isPrimaryIdeal(g);
      const rad = radicalOf(g);
      const isRad = polyEq(rad, g);
      const isUnit = g.length===1 && g[0]===1;
      const isZero = polyEq(g, f);
      const label = isUnit ? '(1)=R' : (isZero ? `(${polyStr(g)})=(0)` : `(${polyStr(g)})`);
      const qStr = isUnit ? '0' : (isZero ? `${fDisplay}` : `${fDisplay}/(${polyStr(g)})`);
      const lines = [];
      lines.push(`ideal (${polyStr(g)}) in R`);
      lines.push(`R/I = 𝔽${p===2?'₂':p===3?'₃':p===5?'₅':p}[x] mod (${polyStr(g)})`);
      const qDeg = g.length-1;
      if(!isUnit) lines.push(`|R/I| = ${p}^${qDeg}`);
      const tags = [];
      if(isMax) tags.push('maximal');
      else if(isPr) tags.push('prime');
      if(isPrimary && !isPr) tags.push('primary');
      if(isRad) tags.push('radical');
      if(tags.length) lines.push('type: '+tags.join(', '));
      return {
        id: 'g'+g.join('_'),
        label, gen: g,
        isPrime: isPr, isMaximal: isMax, isPrimary, isRadical: isRad,
        deg: qDeg,
        info: lines.join('\n'),
      };
    });

    // edges: (g1) contains (g2) iff g1 | g2 (smaller degree bigger ideal)
    const edges = [];
    for(const a of nodes) for(const b of nodes){
      if(a.id===b.id) continue;
      // a.gen divides b.gen AND b.gen/a.gen is irreducible
      const {q,r} = polyDivMod(b.gen, a.gen, p);
      if(polyIsZero(r)){
        // check q is irreducible of positive degree
        if(q.length-1>=1 && isPrimeIdeal(q)){
          edges.push([b.id, a.id]);
        }
      }
    }

    return { nodes, edges, title: `𝔽${p===2?'₂':p===3?'₃':p}[x]/(${fDisplay})`, orderBy:'deg' };
  }

  const PRESETS = {
    'Z/12': ()=>buildZn(12),
    'Z/30': ()=>buildZn(30),
    'Z/36': ()=>buildZn(36),
    'Z/8':  ()=>buildZn(8),
    'F2x/x3+x+1': ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [1,1,0,1], 'x³+x+1'),
    'F2x/x2+x':   ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [0,1,1],   'x²+x'),
    'F3x/x2+1':   ()=>buildPolyQuot(3, [1,0,1],   'x²+1'),
    'F2x/x3':     ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [0,0,0,1], 'x³'),
  };

  let selectedId = null;

  function render(){
    const data = PRESETS[pick.value]();
    if(!data){ info.textContent = 'unavailable'; return; }
    // compute layout by rank (number of prime factors of generator / degree)
    // group nodes by "level" = number of primes in generator (with multiplicity)
    // for Z/n: level = total prime exponent of (n/d) meaning height from bottom
    // simpler: topo rank from bottom
    const { nodes, edges, title } = data;
    const idMap = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>idMap[n.id]=n);
    // compute rank: longest chain from a bottom node (no outgoing edge in 'contained-in' direction)
    // edges are [lower, upper] meaning lower ⊂ upper
    const children = {}, parents = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>{ children[n.id]=[]; parents[n.id]=[]; });
    edges.forEach(([lo,up])=>{
      children[lo].push(up); parents[up].push(lo);
    });
    const rank = {};
    function getRank(id){
      if(rank[id]!==undefined) return rank[id];
      if(parents[id].length===0){ rank[id]=0; return 0; }
      const r = 1 + Math.max(...parents[id].map(getRank));
      rank[id] = r; return r;
    }
    nodes.forEach(n=>getRank(n.id));
    const maxRank = Math.max(...nodes.map(n=>rank[n.id]));
    const layers = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>{
      const r = rank[n.id];
      (layers[r]=layers[r]||[]).push(n);
    });
    // layout
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const W=560, H=420;
    const padT=40, padB=30;
    // draw title
    const t = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:24,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':15,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600});
    t.textContent = title;
    svg.appendChild(t);
    // position
    const pos = {};
    const nLayers = maxRank+1;
    const yStep = (H-padT-padB) / Math.max(nLayers-1, 1);
    for(let r=0;r<=maxRank;r++){
      const layer = layers[r] || [];
      const count = layer.length;
      layer.sort((a,b)=>{
        // sort by generator for stable order
        const ga = typeof a.gen==='number' ? a.gen : a.gen.join(',');
        const gb = typeof b.gen==='number' ? b.gen : b.gen.join(',');
        return ga<gb?-1:ga>gb?1:0;
      });
      layer.forEach((n,i)=>{
        const x = (W/(count+1))*(i+1);
        // bottom = zero ideal (high rank), top = unit (rank 0)
        const y = padT + r*yStep;
        pos[n.id] = [x,y];
      });
    }
    // edges
    for(const [lo,up] of edges){
      const [x1,y1]=pos[lo], [x2,y2]=pos[up];
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    }
    // nodes
    for(const n of nodes){
      const [x,y]=pos[n.id];
      let fill = '#555';
      if(n.isMaximal) fill = 'var(--pink)';
      else if(n.isPrime) fill = 'var(--yellow)';
      else if(n.isPrimary) fill = 'var(--blue)';
      else if(n.isRadical) fill = 'var(--violet)';
      const g = SVG('g',{});
      g.style.cursor='pointer';
      const isSel = selectedId===n.id;
      const c = SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy:y,r:isSel?10:7,fill,stroke:isSel?'#fff':'#111','stroke-width':isSel?2:1});
      const lab = SVG('text',{x:x+12,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:'#e8ecf1'});
      lab.textContent = n.label;
      g.appendChild(c); g.appendChild(lab);
      g.addEventListener('click',()=>{ selectedId=n.id; render(); });
      svg.appendChild(g);
    }
    // info
    const sel = nodes.find(n=>n.id===selectedId) || nodes[0];
    info.textContent = sel.info;
  }

  pick.addEventListener('change',()=>{ selectedId=null; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: In $\mathbb{Z}/n$, the ideals correspond to divisors of $n$: $(d)$ for $d|n$. The prime ideals are $(p)$ for primes $p|n$, and in $\mathbb{Z}/n$ every prime is maximal (Artinian). The nilradical $\mathrm{Nil}(A)=\bigcap\mathfrak{p}$ is the intersection of all primes.

See also
  • Groups, rings, and fields

Used in
Commutative algebra · Integral extensions and Noether normalization
Commutative algebra · Localization and local algebra
Commutative algebra · Modules and tensor products
Commutative algebra · Noetherian rings and Hilbert basis
Commutative algebra · Prime vs maximal ideals
Homological algebra · Chain complexes and homology
… and 3 more.

2. Prime vs maximal ideals

Two distinguished classes of ideals carry almost all the geometric information. An ideal $\mathfrak{p}\subsetneq A$ is prime when $A/\mathfrak{p}$ is an integral domain; maximal when $A/\mathfrak{p}$ is a field. Every maximal ideal is prime (fields are domains), but not vice-versa.

Dimension-zero rings. $A$ is Artinian iff every prime is maximal. Example: $\mathbb{Z}/n$, $k[x]/(f)$, any finite ring. Contrast with $\mathbb{Z}$ or $k[x]$ where $(0)$ is prime but not maximal — the generic point.

Three worked examples to keep next to each other, in increasing dimension:

$\mathbb{Z}$, dim 1 Primes: $(0)$ (generic, height 0) and $(p)$ for $p$ prime (maximal, height 1). Maximals exactly = $\{(p)\}$.

$\mathbb{R}[x]$, dim 1 Primes: $(0)$ (height 0); $(x-a)$ for $a\in\mathbb{R}$ and $(x^2+bx+c)$ with negative discriminant (height 1 maximals with residue field $\mathbb{R}$ or $\mathbb{C}$). All height-$1$ primes are maximal.

$k[x,y]$, dim 2 Three tiers: $(0)$ at height $0$; $(f)$ for $f$ irreducible at height $1$ (curves — prime but not maximal); $(x-a,y-b)$ at height $2$ (points — the maximals, by Hilbert's Nullstellensatz when $k=\overline k$).

    return 'var(--violet)';
  }
  function draw(){
    const R = DATA[pick.value];
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const t = SVG('text',{x:240,y:22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':15,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600});

    for(const p of R.primes){
      const g = SVG('g'); g.style.cursor='pointer';
      const is = sel===p.id;
      const r = p.kind==='generic' ? 10 : (p.kind==='max' ? 7 : 8);
      const c = SVG('circle',{cx:p.pos[0],cy:p.pos[1],r: is?r+3:r, fill:colorOf(p.kind), stroke:is?'#fff':'#111','stroke-width':is?2:1});
      const lab = SVG('text',{x:p.pos[0]+14,y:p.pos[1]+5,'font-size':12,fill:'#e8ecf1'});
      lab.textContent = p.label;
      g.appendChild(c); g.appendChild(lab);
      g.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ sel = p.id; draw(); });
      svg.appendChild(g);
    }
    const cur = R.primes.find(p=>p.id===sel) || R.primes[0];
    info.textContent = `${cur.label}\n${cur.desc}`;
  }
  pick.addEventListener('change', ()=>{ sel=null; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

    if(k==='prime') return 'var(--yellow)';
    return 'var(--violet)';
  }
  function draw(){
    const R = DATA[pick.value];
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const t = SVG('text',{x:240,y:22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':15,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600});
    t.textContent = R.title; svg.appendChild(t);
    for(const p of R.primes){
      const g = SVG('g'); g.style.cursor='pointer';
      const is = sel===p.id;
      const r = p.kind==='generic' ? 10 : (p.kind==='max' ? 7 : 8);
      const c = SVG('circle',{cx:p.pos[0],cy:p.pos[1],r: is?r+3:r, fill:colorOf(p.kind), stroke:is?'#fff':'#111','stroke-width':is?2:1});
      const lab = SVG('text',{x:p.pos[0]+14,y:p.pos[1]+5,'font-size':12,fill:'#e8ecf1'});
      lab.textContent = p.label;
      g.appendChild(c); g.appendChild(lab);
      g.addEventListener('click', ()=>{ sel = p.id; draw(); });
      svg.appendChild(g);
    }
    const cur = R.primes.find(p=>p.id===sel) || R.primes[0];
    info.textContent = `${cur.label}\n${cur.desc}`;
  }
  pick.addEventListener('change', ()=>{ sel=null; draw(); });
  draw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
3. Spec and the Zariski topology

Given a ring $A$, $\mathrm{Spec}(A)$ becomes a topological space with closed sets

$V(I) = \{\mathfrak{p}\in\mathrm{Spec}(A) : I\subseteq\mathfrak{p}\}.$

Open sets have a particularly clean basis: distinguished opens

$D(f) = \{\mathfrak{p} : f\notin\mathfrak{p}\} = \mathrm{Spec}(A_f).$

Two features make this topology unusual — and useful:

  • Points are generally not closed. The closure of $\{\mathfrak{p}\}$ is $V(\mathfrak{p})$; maximal ideals are closed, but $(0)\in\mathrm{Spec}(\mathbb{Z})$ has closure = whole Spec.
  • $\mathrm{Spec}(A)$ is always quasi-compact, though rarely Hausdorff. Connectedness corresponds algebraically to idempotents: $A = A_1 \times A_2 \iff \mathrm{Spec}(A)$ disconnected.

Irreducible components of $\mathrm{Spec}(A)$ biject with minimal primes of $A$: each minimal prime $\mathfrak{p}$ gives the irreducible closed set $V(\mathfrak{p})$.

  • Topological spaces

blocks.html:
4. Nilradical and Jacobson radical

Two canonical ideals measure different flavors of "smallness":

$$\mathrm{nil}(A) = \bigcap_{\mathfrak{p}\text{ prime}}\mathfrak{p} = \{x\in A : x^n = 0\text{ for some }n\}.$$ $$\mathrm{Jac}(A) = \bigcap_{\mathfrak{m}\text{ maximal}}\mathfrak{m} = \{x\in A : 1 - xy\in A^\times\text{ for all }y\in A\}.$$

Both are ideals; $\mathrm{nil}(A)\subseteq\mathrm{Jac}(A)$ always, since maximal $\Rightarrow$ prime. The two coincide in Jacobson rings (e.g. any finitely generated $k$-algebra, or $\mathbb{Z}$): $\mathrm{nil}(A/I) = \mathrm{Jac}(A/I)$ for all ideals $I$. A ring with $\mathrm{nil}(A) = 0$ is called reduced.

Examples:

$\mathbb{Z}/72$ $72 = 2^3\cdot 3^2$. Nilradical = $(6)$, Jacobson radical = $(6)$ (Artinian: agree).

$\mathbb{Z}$ reduced, $\mathrm{nil} = (0)$. Jacobson: $\bigcap_p (p) = (0)$ too — $\mathbb{Z}$ is Jacobson.

$\mathbb{Z}_{(p)}$ $\mathrm{nil}=(0)$ (local domain) but $\mathrm{Jac} = (p)\ne 0$. Not Jacobson.

(function(){
  function radOfN(n){
    let r = 1;
    for(const [p] of factorizeInt(n)) r *= p;
    return r;
  }
  const PRESETS = {
    'Z72': ()=>{
      const n = 72; const r = radOfN(n);
      return {
        title:'ℤ/72',
        primes: factorizeInt(n).map(([p])=>`(${p})`).join(', '),
        max: factorizeInt(n).map(([p])=>`(${p})`).join(', '),

        jac: `(${r}) = (6) — agrees with nilradical (Artinian)`,
        reduced: r===1
      };
    },
    'Z100': ()=>{
      const n=100; const r=radOfN(n);
      return { title:'ℤ/100', primes:'(2), (5)', max:'(2), (5)',
        nil:`(${r}) = (10) — nilpotent = divisible by 10`,
        jac:`(${r}) = (10) — same (Artinian)`, reduced:r===1 };
    },
    'Z30': ()=>({ title:'ℤ/30', primes:'(2), (3), (5)', max:'(2), (3), (5)',
      nil:'(30)=(0) — no nilpotents beyond 0; ℤ/30 is REDUCED',
      jac:'(30)=(0) — intersection of all three maximals is 0',
      reduced:true }),
    'kxy-x2': ()=>({ title:'k[x,y]/(x²)', primes:'(x) (unique minimal)', max:'(x, y−a) for a∈k',
      nil:'(x) = the nilpotent ideal; x²=0',
      jac:'(x) — agrees (Jacobson ring)',
      reduced:false }),
    'kxy-x2y2': ()=>({ title:'k[x,y]/(x²,y²)', primes:'(x,y) unique', max:'(x,y)',
      nil:'(x,y) — both x,y nilpotent',
      jac:'(x,y) — same (Artinian local)',
      reduced:false }),
    'Z': ()=>({ title:'ℤ', primes:'(0), (p) for all primes p', max:'(p) for primes p',
      nil:'(0) — ℤ is REDUCED',
      jac:'∩_p (p) = (0) — ℤ is Jacobson',
      reduced:true }),
    'Zp': ()=>({ title:'ℤ_(p) · local at p', primes:'(0), (p)', max:'(p) only',
      nil:'(0) — domain',
      jac:'(p) — ≠ nilradical! ℤ_(p) is NOT Jacobson',
      reduced:true })
  };
  const pick = $('#rad-ring'), out = $('#rad-out');
  function draw(){
    const R = PRESETS[pick.value]();
    let msg = `ring: ${R.title}\n\nprimes: ${R.primes}\nmaximals: ${R.max}\n\n`;
    msg += `nilradical = ${R.nil}\n`;
    msg += `Jacobson = ${R.jac}\n\n`;
    msg += R.reduced ? '→ REDUCED (no nonzero nilpotents)' : '→ NOT reduced';
    out.textContent = msg;
  }
  pick.addEventListener('change', draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Nilradical is "algebraic information you can't see geometrically" — reducing a scheme kills nilpotents but keeps the underlying point set. Jacobson radical is what Nakayama's lemma handles in §9.

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 1: ideal lattice =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#lat-svg'), pick = $('#lat-ring'), info = $('#lat-info');

  /* build lattice data from ring spec */
  function buildZn(n){
    const divs = divisors(n);
    // ideal (d) corresponds to d | n; (d)=(e) iff d=e up to unit, here d is the generator
    // lattice: (d1) subseteq (d2) iff d2 | d1
    const primes = new Set(factorizeInt(n).map(([p])=>p));
    const nodes = divs.map(d=>{
      const quot = n/d; // |R/I| = d? No: ideal (d) in Z/n has size n/d so |R/(d)| = d
      // actually: (d) as subset of Z/n equals dZ/nZ, which has n/d elements; quotient has d elements
      const quotSize = d;
      // Is (d) prime in Z/n? Iff Z/n / (d) = Z/d is a domain, iff d is prime or d=1? d=1 gives (1)=R, not prime.
      // Actually quotient Z/n /(d) as a group is Z/d if d | n. Is it a domain? It's Z/d, which is a domain iff d=0 or d prime. d ranges in divisors of n, so d>=1.
      // So prime iff d is prime. Maximal iff Z/d is a field iff d prime. So prime=maximal here (Artinian).
      const isPrime = primes.has(d);
      const isMaximal = isPrime;
      // primary: (d) primary iff quotient has only one associated prime; in Z/n, (d) primary iff d is a prime power
      const isPrimePower = (d>1) && factorizeInt(d).length===1;
      const isPrimary = isPrimePower;
      // radical of (d) in Z/n: rad = product of distinct primes dividing d (intersected with the primes of n)
      let rad = 1;
      for(const [p] of factorizeInt(d)) rad *= p;
      // but rad in Z/n means smallest d' | n with same prime support as d:
      let radGen = rad;
      // if d=1 -> rad = n (the full ideal? No, rad of (1) in Z/n... (1)=unit ideal so rad=(1))
      if(d===1) radGen = 1;
      else {
        // adjust: radGen should divide n
        while(n%radGen!==0) radGen++;
      }
      const isRadical = (d===radGen);
      return {
        id: `d${d}`,
        label: d===1 ? '(1)=R' : (d===n ? `(${d})=(0)` : `(${d})`),
        gen: d,
        quotSize,
        isPrime, isMaximal, isPrimary, isRadical,
        info: (()=>{
          const lines = [];
          lines.push(`ideal (${d}) in ℤ/${n}`);
          lines.push(`quotient R/I ≅ ℤ/${d}`);
          lines.push(`|R/I| = ${d}`);
          if(d===1) lines.push('this is the unit ideal');
          if(d===n) lines.push('this is the zero ideal');
          const tags = [];
          if(isMaximal) tags.push('maximal');
          else if(isPrime) tags.push('prime');
          if(isPrimary && !isPrime) tags.push('primary');
          if(isRadical) tags.push('radical');
          if(tags.length) lines.push('type: ' + tags.join(', '));
          return lines.join('\n');
        })(),
      };
    });
    // edges: (d1) directly contains (d2) means d2 is a multiple of d1 and d2/d1 is prime
    const edges = [];
    for(const a of nodes) for(const b of nodes){
      if(a.gen!==b.gen && b.gen%a.gen===0){
        const q = b.gen/a.gen;
        if(isPrime(q)){
          // a ⊇ b (bigger ideal contains smaller)
          edges.push([b.id, a.id]); // draw from smaller (lower) to bigger (upper)
        }
      }
    }
    return { nodes, edges, title:`ℤ/${n}ℤ`, orderBy:'gen' };
  }

  function buildPolyQuot(p, f, fDisplay){
    // ring R = F_p[x] / (f). Ideals correspond to monic divisors g | f.
    // enumerate all monic divisors of f by trying all monic polys of degree 0..deg f
    const d = f.length-1;
    if(d>4){ return null; }
    // enumerate all monic polys of degree 1..d, and check divides f
    const divs = [];
    // divisor (1) i.e. the unit ideal: generator 1
    divs.push([1]);
    for(let dg=1; dg<=d; dg++){
      // iterate over leading-coeff-1 polys of degree dg
      const coefs = Array(dg).fill(0);
      function rec(i){
        if(i===dg){
          const g = coefs.concat([1]); // leading 1
          const {q,r} = polyDivMod(f, g, p);
          if(polyIsZero(r)){
            divs.push(polyTrim(g));
          }
          return;
        }
        for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[i]=c; rec(i+1); }
      }
      rec(0);
    }
    // dedupe
    const seen = new Set();
    const divs2 = [];
    for(const g of divs){
      const k = g.join(',');
      if(!seen.has(k)){ seen.add(k); divs2.push(g); }
    }
    // For each divisor g, the ideal (g) in R satisfies (g) ⊇ (h) iff g | h
    // primes of R are (g) for g monic irreducible factor of f
    // need to factor f into irreducibles to identify primes
    // factorization: try all monic polys of degree 1..d/2 and see if divides
    function factorIrreducible(a){
      // returns list of [irred factor, multiplicity]
      const out = [];
      const da = a.length-1;
      let cur = a.slice();
      for(let dg=1; dg<=da; dg++){
        // iterate monics of degree dg
        let found = true;
        while(found){
          found = false;
          const coefs = Array(dg).fill(0);
          let chosen = null;
          function rec(i){
            if(chosen) return;
            if(i===dg){
              const g = coefs.concat([1]);
              const {q,r} = polyDivMod(cur, g, p);
              if(polyIsZero(r) && g.length-1===dg){
                // check g is irreducible: no divisor of degree 1..dg/2
                let irr = true;
                if(dg>1){
                  for(let dgg=1; dgg<=dg/2 && irr; dgg++){
                    const coefs2 = Array(dgg).fill(0);
                    function rec2(j){
                      if(!irr) return;
                      if(j===dgg){
                        const h = coefs2.concat([1]);
                        const {r:rr2} = polyDivMod(g, h, p);
                        if(polyIsZero(rr2)) irr = false;
                        return;
                      }
                      for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs2[j]=c; rec2(j+1); }
                    }
                    rec2(0);
                  }
                }
                if(irr) chosen = g;
              }
              return;
            }
            for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[i]=c; rec(i+1); }
          }
          rec(0);
          if(chosen){
            const {q} = polyDivMod(cur, chosen, p);
            cur = q;
            // add to out
            const existing = out.find(e=>polyEq(e[0], chosen));
            if(existing) existing[1]++;
            else out.push([chosen, 1]);
            found = true;
            if(cur.length-1 < dg) break;
          }
        }
      }
      return out;
    }
    const fFact = factorIrreducible(f);

    function isPrimeIdeal(g){
      // (g) prime iff g is irreducible (in R = F_p[x]/(f), ideal (g) where g | f)
      // (g) ≠ R requires g != unit
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return false;
      if(g.length-1===0) return false;
      // g is irreducible iff no divisor of degree 1..deg(g)/2
      const dg = g.length-1;
      for(let dgg=1; dgg<=dg/2; dgg++){
        const coefs = Array(dgg).fill(0);
        let flag = false;
        function rec(j){
          if(flag) return;
          if(j===dgg){
            const h = coefs.concat([1]);
            const {r:rr} = polyDivMod(g, h, p);
            if(polyIsZero(rr) && g.length-1 > dgg) flag = true;
            return;
          }
          for(let c=0;c<p;c++){ coefs[j]=c; rec(j+1); }
        }
        rec(0);
        if(flag) return false;
      }
      return true;
    }

    function isPrimaryIdeal(g){
      // in R = F_p[x]/(f), (g) primary iff g is a power of an irreducible
      // meaning g = q^k for some irreducible q
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return false;
      if(g.length-1===0) return false;
      // factor g
      const gf = factorIrreducible(g);
      return gf.length===1;
    }

    function radicalOf(g){
      // radical in R: product of distinct irreducible factors, intersected with structure.
      // Here rad((g)) = (square-free part of g, reduced mod f)
      // But in R = F_p[x]/(f), we only consider g | f, so factors are among factors of f.
      if(g.length===1 && g[0]===1) return [1];
      const gf = factorIrreducible(g);
      let r = [1];
      for(const [q] of gf) r = polyMul(r, q, p);
      // reduce mod f just in case (though r | f)
      return polyMod(r, f, p);
    }

    const nodes = divs2.map(g=>{
      const isPr = isPrimeIdeal(g);
      // maximal iff quotient is a field; R/(g) as F_p[x]/(g)... but R = F_p[x]/(f), so R/(g) = F_p[x]/(g) when g|f
      // R/(g) field iff g irreducible. So maximal iff prime (true in this Artinian quotient).
      const isMax = isPr;
      const isPrimary = isPrimaryIdeal(g);
      const rad = radicalOf(g);
      const isRad = polyEq(rad, g);
      const isUnit = g.length===1 && g[0]===1;
      const isZero = polyEq(g, f);
      const label = isUnit ? '(1)=R' : (isZero ? `(${polyStr(g)})=(0)` : `(${polyStr(g)})`);
      const qStr = isUnit ? '0' : (isZero ? `${fDisplay}` : `${fDisplay}/(${polyStr(g)})`);
      const lines = [];
      lines.push(`ideal (${polyStr(g)}) in R`);
      lines.push(`R/I = 𝔽${p===2?'₂':p===3?'₃':p===5?'₅':p}[x] mod (${polyStr(g)})`);
      const qDeg = g.length-1;
      if(!isUnit) lines.push(`|R/I| = ${p}^${qDeg}`);
      const tags = [];
      if(isMax) tags.push('maximal');
      else if(isPr) tags.push('prime');
      if(isPrimary && !isPr) tags.push('primary');
      if(isRad) tags.push('radical');
      if(tags.length) lines.push('type: '+tags.join(', '));
      return {
        id: 'g'+g.join('_'),
        label, gen: g,
        isPrime: isPr, isMaximal: isMax, isPrimary, isRadical: isRad,
        deg: qDeg,
        info: lines.join('\n'),
      };
    });

    // edges: (g1) contains (g2) iff g1 | g2 (smaller degree bigger ideal)
    const edges = [];
    for(const a of nodes) for(const b of nodes){
      if(a.id===b.id) continue;
      // a.gen divides b.gen AND b.gen/a.gen is irreducible
      const {q,r} = polyDivMod(b.gen, a.gen, p);
      if(polyIsZero(r)){
        // check q is irreducible of positive degree
        if(q.length-1>=1 && isPrimeIdeal(q)){
          edges.push([b.id, a.id]);
        }
      }
    }

    return { nodes, edges, title: `𝔽${p===2?'₂':p===3?'₃':p}[x]/(${fDisplay})`, orderBy:'deg' };
  }

  const PRESETS = {
    'Z/12': ()=>buildZn(12),
    'Z/30': ()=>buildZn(30),
    'Z/36': ()=>buildZn(36),
    'Z/8':  ()=>buildZn(8),
    'F2x/x3+x+1': ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [1,1,0,1], 'x³+x+1'),
    'F2x/x2+x':   ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [0,1,1],   'x²+x'),
    'F3x/x2+1':   ()=>buildPolyQuot(3, [1,0,1],   'x²+1'),
    'F2x/x3':     ()=>buildPolyQuot(2, [0,0,0,1], 'x³'),
  };

  let selectedId = null;

  function render(){
    const data = PRESETS[pick.value]();
    if(!data){ info.textContent = 'unavailable'; return; }
    // compute layout by rank (number of prime factors of generator / degree)
    // group nodes by "level" = number of primes in generator (with multiplicity)
    // for Z/n: level = total prime exponent of (n/d) meaning height from bottom
    // simpler: topo rank from bottom
    const { nodes, edges, title } = data;
    const idMap = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>idMap[n.id]=n);
    // compute rank: longest chain from a bottom node (no outgoing edge in 'contained-in' direction)
    // edges are [lower, upper] meaning lower ⊂ upper
    const children = {}, parents = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>{ children[n.id]=[]; parents[n.id]=[]; });
    edges.forEach(([lo,up])=>{
      children[lo].push(up); parents[up].push(lo);
    });
    const rank = {};
    function getRank(id){
      if(rank[id]!==undefined) return rank[id];
      if(parents[id].length===0){ rank[id]=0; return 0; }
      const r = 1 + Math.max(...parents[id].map(getRank));
      rank[id] = r; return r;
    }
    nodes.forEach(n=>getRank(n.id));
    const maxRank = Math.max(...nodes.map(n=>rank[n.id]));
    const layers = {};
    nodes.forEach(n=>{
      const r = rank[n.id];
      (layers[r]=layers[r]||[]).push(n);
    });
    // layout
    svg.innerHTML = '';
    const W=560, H=420;
    const padT=40, padB=30;
    // draw title
    const t = SVG('text',{x:W/2,y:24,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':15,fill:'#fff','font-weight':600});
    t.textContent = title;
    svg.appendChild(t);
    // position
    const pos = {};
    const nLayers = maxRank+1;
    const yStep = (H-padT-padB) / Math.max(nLayers-1, 1);
    for(let r=0;r<=maxRank;r++){
      const layer = layers[r] || [];
      const count = layer.length;
      layer.sort((a,b)=>{
        // sort by generator for stable order
        const ga = typeof a.gen==='number' ? a.gen : a.gen.join(',');
        const gb = typeof b.gen==='number' ? b.gen : b.gen.join(',');
        return ga<gb?-1:ga>gb?1:0;
      });
      layer.forEach((n,i)=>{
        const x = (W/(count+1))*(i+1);
        // bottom = zero ideal (high rank), top = unit (rank 0)
        const y = padT + r*yStep;
        pos[n.id] = [x,y];
      });
    }
    // edges
    for(const [lo,up] of edges){
      const [x1,y1]=pos[lo], [x2,y2]=pos[up];
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1,y1,x2,y2,stroke:'var(--line)','stroke-width':1.2}));
    }
    // nodes
    for(const n of nodes){
      const [x,y]=pos[n.id];
      let fill = '#555';
      if(n.isMaximal) fill = 'var(--pink)';
      else if(n.isPrime) fill = 'var(--yellow)';
      else if(n.isPrimary) fill = 'var(--blue)';
      else if(n.isRadical) fill = 'var(--violet)';
      const g = SVG('g',{});
      g.style.cursor='pointer';
      const isSel = selectedId===n.id;
      const c = SVG('circle',{cx:x,cy:y,r:isSel?10:7,fill,stroke:isSel?'#fff':'#111','stroke-width':isSel?2:1});
      const lab = SVG('text',{x:x+12,y:y+4,'font-size':12,fill:'#e8ecf1'});
      lab.textContent = n.label;
      g.appendChild(c); g.appendChild(lab);
      g.addEventListener('click',()=>{ selectedId=n.id; render(); });
      svg.appendChild(g);
    }
    // info
    const sel = nodes.find(n=>n.id===selectedId) || nodes[0];
    info.textContent = sel.info;
  }

  pick.addEventListener('change',()=>{ selectedId=null; render(); });
  render();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
5. Modules and tensor products

A module $M$ over $A$ is an abelian group with an $A$-action — "linear algebra over rings". When $A$ is a field this is just a vector space; when $A=\mathbb{Z}$ it is an abelian group. The interesting thing is that over $\mathbb{Z}$, unlike over a field, modules can have torsion: $2\cdot \bar 1 = 0$ in $\mathbb{Z}/2$.

The tensor product $M\otimes_A N$ is the universal object through which $A$-bilinear maps $M\times N\to P$ factor. Two operational rules do all the work:

$$(m_1+m_2)\otimes n = m_1\otimes n + m_2\otimes n, \qquad (am)\otimes n = m\otimes (an).$$ Consequence: $\mathbb{Z}/m \otimes_\mathbb{Z} \mathbb{Z}/n \;=\; \mathbb{Z}/\gcd(m,n)$. Because $n\cdot (1\otimes 1) = 1\otimes n = 0$ and $m\cdot(1\otimes 1)=0$.

For f.g. abelian groups, tensor is additive in each slot; so by the structure theorem you can tensor piece-by-piece. This is a computation, not a theory.

params.bodyMarkup:
Tensor product over ℤ
enter M and N as products of cyclic groups
syntax: Z + Z/4 + Z/6
params.bodyScript:
<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 2: tensor product =============================== */
(function(){
  function parseGroup(s){
    // input like "Z + Z/4 + Z/6" -> list of orders; 0 means Z (free)
    return s.split('+').map(x=>x.trim()).filter(Boolean).map(tok=>{
      if(tok==='Z') return 0;
      const m = tok.match(/^Z\/(\d+)$/);
      if(!m) return null;
      const v = parseInt(m[1],10);
      if(!isFinite(v) || v<1) return null;
      return v;
    });
  }
  function fmt(ords){
    // format list of orders into a string
    if(ords.length===0) return '0';
    const frees = ords.filter(x=>x===0).length;
    const tors = ords.filter(x=>x>0).slice().sort((a,b)=>a-b);
    const parts = [];
    if(frees===1) parts.push('ℤ');
    else if(frees>1) parts.push(`ℤ^${frees}`);
    for(const t of tors) parts.push(`ℤ/${t}`);
    return parts.join(' ⊕ ');
  }
  function tensor(ms, ns){
    // ms ⊗ ns = direct sum of (m_i ⊗ n_j)
    // rules: 0 ⊗ x = x (Z tensor X = X, both as Z and as Z/n)
    //         m ⊗ n = gcd(m,n)-torsion (if m,n > 0)
    //         0 ⊗ 0 = 0 (Z tensor Z = Z) => represent as 0 in list
    const out = [];
    for(const a of ms) for(const b of ns){
      if(a===0 && b===0) out.push(0);
      else if(a===0) out.push(b);
      else if(b===0) out.push(a);
      else out.push(gcd(a,b));
    }
    // drop zeros of the form Z/1 (trivial)
    return out.filter(x=>x!==1);
  }
  function snfLike(ords){
    // put into "canonical" form: list of invariant factors d1 | d2 | ... plus free rank
    const frees = ords.filter(x=>x===0).length;
    const tors = ords.filter(x=>x>0);
    // combine cyclic factors into invariant factors via primary decomposition
    // collect prime-power components
    const primePowers = {};
    for(const t of tors){
      for(const [p,e] of factorizeInt(t)){
        const pp = Math.pow(p,e);
        primePowers[p] = primePowers[p] || [];
        primePowers[p].push(pp);
      }
    }
    // sort each prime's list descending
    for(const p in primePowers) primePowers[p].sort((a,b)=>b-a);
    // number of invariant factors = max length
    let maxLen = 0;
    for(const p in primePowers) maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, primePowers[p].length);
    const inv = Array(maxLen).fill(1);
    for(const p in primePowers){
      const list = primePowers[p];
      for(let i=0;i<list.length;i++){
        inv[i] *= list[i];
      }
    }
    // filter 1's
    return { free: frees, inv: inv.filter(x=>x>1) };
  }
  function fmtSNF(snf){
    const parts = [];
    for(const d of snf.inv) parts.push(`ℤ/${d}`);
    if(snf.free===1) parts.push('ℤ');
    else if(snf.free>1) parts.push(`ℤ^${snf.free}`);
    if(parts.length===0) return '0';
    return parts.join(' ⊕ ');
  }

  const mi = $('#t-m'), ni = $('#t-n'), out = $('#t-out'), go = $('#t-go');
  function compute(){
    const ms = parseGroup(mi.value), ns = parseGroup(ni.value);
    if(!ms || ms.some(x=>x===null) || !ns || ns.some(x=>x===null)){
      out.textContent = 'parse error — use "Z", "Z/n", and "+"';
      return;
    }
    const result = tensor(ms, ns);
    const snf = snfLike(result);
    let msg = '';
    msg += `M = ${fmt(ms)}\n`;
    msg += `N = ${fmt(ns)}\n\n`;
    msg += `M ⊗_ℤ N, expanded bilinearly:\n`;
    const lines = [];
    for(const a of ms) for(const b of ns){
      const left = a===0 ? 'ℤ' : `ℤ/${a}`;
      const right = b===0 ? 'ℤ' : `ℤ/${b}`;
      let res;
      if(a===0 && b===0) res = 'ℤ';
      else if(a===0) res = b===1 ? '0' : `ℤ/${b}`;
      else if(b===0) res = a===1 ? '0' : `ℤ/${a}`;
      else { const g = gcd(a,b); res = g===1 ? '0' : `ℤ/${g}`; }
      lines.push(`  ${left} ⊗ ${right} = ${res}`);
    }
    msg += lines.join('\n');
    msg += `\n\nsum = ${fmt(result)}\n`;
    msg += `\nin invariant-factor form:\n  M ⊗ N ≅ ${fmtSNF(snf)}`;
    out.textContent = msg;
  }
  go.addEventListener('click', compute);
  mi.addEventListener('keydown', e=>{ if(e.key==='Enter') compute(); });
  ni.addEventListener('keydown', e=>{ if(e.key==='Enter') compute(); });
  $$('.preset', $('#w-tens')).forEach(b=>{
    b.addEventListener('click',()=>{
      mi.value = b.dataset.m; ni.value = b.dataset.n; compute();
    });
  });
  compute();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
Two things to notice. (i) Tensoring with $\mathbb{Q}$ kills torsion: $\mathbb{Z}/n\otimes_\mathbb{Z}\mathbb{Q}=0$. (ii) $\mathbb{Z}\otimes_\mathbb{Z}\mathbb{Z}/n=\mathbb{Z}/n$ (identity). Over $\mathbb{Z}$ the tensor product is not left-exact — we'll see this bite in §8.

Submodules, direct sums, exact sequences

Submodules $N\subseteq M$ and quotients $M/N$ work exactly as for groups. Direct sums $M\oplus N$ exist for any family. A sequence $L\xrightarrow{f}M\xrightarrow{g}N$ is exact at $M$ when $\ker g = \mathrm{im}\,f$; a short exact sequence $0\to A\to B\to C\to 0$ packages "$A$ is a submodule with quotient $C$". For $\mathbb{Z}$-modules, $0\to\mathbb{Z}/2\to\mathbb{Z}/6\to\mathbb{Z}/3\to 0$ is exact and splits (since $\gcd(2,3)=1$), so $\mathbb{Z}/6\cong\mathbb{Z}/2\oplus\mathbb{Z}/3$.

A key identity for tensor: $A/I\otimes_A A/J\cong A/(I+J)$. Specialized to $A=\mathbb{Z}$: $\mathbb{Z}/m\otimes\mathbb{Z}/n\cong\mathbb{Z}/\gcd(m,n)$, exactly the widget above.

Used in
Commutative algebra · Flatness and Tor
Commutative algebra · Nakayama's lemma and Zariski tangent spaces
Commutative algebra · Noetherian rings and Hilbert basis

blocks.html:
6. Noetherian rings and the Hilbert basis theorem

A ring $A$ is Noetherian when every ascending chain of ideals $I_1\subseteq I_2\subseteq\cdots$ eventually stabilizes — equivalently, every ideal is finitely generated. This bounds "how complicated" ideals can be and is the single hypothesis most commutative-algebra theorems actually need.

Hilbert basis theorem. If $A$ is Noetherian, so is $A[x]$. Iterating: $k[x_1,\dots,x_n]$ is Noetherian over any field $k$; every finitely generated $k$-algebra is Noetherian; $\mathbb{Z}[x_1,\dots,x_n]$ is Noetherian. All the rings you meet in daily algebra are Noetherian — failures are deliberate constructions ($k[x_1,x_2,\dots]$ in infinitely many variables, $C(\mathbb{R})$, huge direct products).

Three equivalent conditions on $A$:

ACC every ascending chain of ideals stabilizes.

f.g. every ideal is finitely generated.

maximum every nonempty set of ideals has a maximal element.

The same three conditions define "Noetherian $A$-module" (replacing "ideal" with "submodule"). A submodule or quotient of a Noetherian module is Noetherian; extensions stay Noetherian; so finite direct sums and f.g. modules over a Noetherian ring are Noetherian.

blocks.html:
7. Artinian local rings

A ring $A$ is Artinian when it satisfies the descending chain condition (DCC): every chain of ideals $I_1\supseteq I_2\supseteq\cdots$ eventually stabilizes. The dual of Noetherianness — but the two are not symmetric. The Akizuki–Hopkins theorem gives a much sharper picture:

Akizuki–Hopkins. A commutative ring is Artinian iff it is Noetherian and every prime ideal is maximal (Krull dimension $0$). Equivalently, $A$ has finite length as a module over itself: $\ell_A(A)<\infty$.

So Artinian = Noetherian + dimension zero. In particular every Artinian ring is finite-length, every prime is maximal, and the structure theorem says

$A \;\cong\; \prod_{i=1}^r A_{\mathfrak{m}_i}$

is a finite product of Artinian local rings — one factor per maximal ideal. So the Artinian local rings are the irreducible building blocks; understanding them gives every Artinian ring.

An Artinian local $k$-algebra is an Artinian local ring $(A,\mathfrak{m})$ together with an isomorphism $A/\mathfrak{m} \cong k$ (residue field equal to $k$). The maximal ideal is then automatically nilpotent — there exists $n$ with $\mathfrak{m}^n = 0$ — and $A$ is finite-dimensional as a $k$-vector space with $\dim_k A = \ell_A(A)$. Three families to keep close at hand:

$k[\epsilon]/(\epsilon^n)$ the truncated polynomial ring. Length $n$, basis $\{1,\epsilon,\dots,\epsilon^{n-1}\}$. The case $n=2$ — the dual numbers — is the universal "first-order thickening" of $\mathrm{Spec}\,k$.

$k[x,y]/(x,y)^n$ $n$-th order thickening of the origin in $\mathbb{A}^2_k$. Length $\binom{n+1}{2}$.

$k[x,y]/(x^2,xy,y^2)$ a "fat point" with two-dimensional Zariski tangent space — same length as $k[\epsilon]/(\epsilon^2) \times_k k[\epsilon]/(\epsilon^2)$ but a different ring, distinguishing tangent directions.

Why deformation theory cares. A first-order deformation of a $k$-scheme $X_0$ is a flat lift to $\mathrm{Spec}\,k[\epsilon]/(\epsilon^2)$. Higher-order deformations probe lifts to $k[\epsilon]/(\epsilon^n)$, $k[x,y]/(x,y)^n$, and the entire category $\mathrm{Art}_k$ of Artinian local $k$-algebras with residue field $k$. Schlessinger's theorem states a representability criterion for the functor $\mathrm{Def}_{X_0}: \mathrm{Art}_k \to \mathbf{Set}$ in exactly this language — and his "test rings" are the small surjections $A' \twoheadrightarrow A$ inside $\mathrm{Art}_k$.

blocks.html:
8. Localization

Localization is the algebraic shadow of "zooming in". Pick a multiplicatively closed set $S\subset A$ (with $1\in S$, closed under multiplication, not containing $0$ unless you want to kill the ring). The localization

$S^{-1}A = \{a/s : a\in A, s\in S\} / \sim, \qquad a/s \sim a'/s' \iff \exists t\in S,\; t(a s' - a' s) = 0.$

The extra "$t$" is there to handle zero-divisors. Two important special cases:

$A_f$ invert a single element $f$. Geometrically: remove $V(f)$ from $\mathrm{Spec}$. $\mathrm{Spec}(A_f) = D(f)$.

$A_\mathfrak{p}$ invert everything outside a prime $\mathfrak{p}$. The result is a local ring: one maximal ideal, namely $\mathfrak{p}A_\mathfrak{p}$. This is the local ring "at the point $\mathfrak{p}$".

Primes of $S^{-1}A$ = primes of $A$ disjoint from $S$. Hence $\mathrm{Spec}(A_\mathfrak{p})=\{\mathfrak{q}\subseteq\mathfrak{p}\}$ and $\mathrm{Spec}(A_f)=\{\mathfrak{q}:f\notin\mathfrak{q}\}$. The residue field $A_\mathfrak{p}/\mathfrak{p}A_\mathfrak{p} = \mathrm{Frac}(A/\mathfrak{p})$ is the "function field of the stalk".

params.bodyMarkup:
Localization computer
pick a ring, pick what to invert
params.bodyScript:
<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 3: localization =============================== */
(function(){
  const ringSel = $('#loc-ring'), modeSel = $('#loc-mode'),
        eltIn = $('#loc-elt'), out = $('#loc-out');

  function compute(){
    const ring = ringSel.value, mode = modeSel.value;
    let elt = parseInt(eltIn.value,10);
    if(!isFinite(elt)){ out.textContent='bad element'; return; }
    const lines = [];

    if(ring==='Z'){
      lines.push('A = ℤ');
      if(mode==='f'){
        if(elt===0){ lines.push('inverting 0 kills everything: ℤ[1/0] = 0'); out.textContent=lines.join('\n'); return; }
        lines.push(`S = {1, ${elt}, ${elt}², …}`);
        const fact = factorizeInt(Math.abs(elt));
        const pstr = fact.map(([p,e])=>e===1?`${p}`:`${p}^${e}`).join(' · ') || '1';
        lines.push(`S^{-1}ℤ = ℤ[1/${elt}]`);
        if(fact.length){
          lines.push(`        = ℤ[1/${fact.map(f=>f[0]).join(', 1/')}]`);
        }
        lines.push(`Spec = Spec(ℤ) − V(${elt}) = {(p) : p prime, p ∤ ${elt}}`);
        lines.push(`removed points: ${fact.map(f=>`(${f[0]})`).join(', ') || 'none'}`);
        lines.push('local? no (infinitely many primes remain)');
      } else {
        if(elt===0){
          lines.push('S = ℤ − (0) = all non-zero integers');
          lines.push('S^{-1}ℤ = ℚ (fraction field)');
          lines.push('this is ℤ localized at the generic point (0)');
          lines.push('local ring: maximal ideal (0), residue field ℚ');
        } else if(isPrime(Math.abs(elt))){
          const p = Math.abs(elt);
          lines.push(`𝔭 = (${p})`);
          lines.push(`S = ℤ − (${p}) = {integers coprime to ${p}}`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}ℤ = ℤ_{(${p})} = {a/b : p ∤ b}`);
          lines.push(`this is a DVR — local, maximal ideal (${p}), residue field 𝔽${p}`);
          lines.push(`Spec = {(0), (${p})} — the generic point and the closed point`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`(${elt}) is not prime in ℤ — pick a prime or 0`);
        }
      }
    } else {
      // Z/n
      const n = ring==='Z12'?12: ring==='Z30'?30: 6;
      lines.push(`A = ℤ/${n}`);
      if(mode==='f'){
        const a = mod(elt, n);
        const g = gcd(a, n);
        lines.push(`invert ${a} ∈ ℤ/${n}`);
        lines.push(`S = {1, ${a}, ${a}², …} mod ${n}`);
        // In Z/n, inverting a: a has finite powers, eventually periodic. S^{-1}A = A/(ann) essentially.
        // S^{-1}(Z/n) = Z/(n/gcd(n,a^∞)) where gcd(n,a^∞) = radical stuff.
        // Actually: inverting a in Z/n gives Z/n[1/a]. Let d = gcd(n, a^k) for large k.
        // Result: Z/n localized at {powers of a} = Z/(n/d), where d = largest divisor of n whose prime factors divide a.
        let d = 1;
        // primes of n dividing a:
        for(const [p] of factorizeInt(n)){
          if(a % p === 0){
            // find largest power of p dividing n
            let q = 1; while(n % (q*p) === 0) q *= p;
            d *= q;
          }
        }
        const m = n/d;
        if(m===1){
          lines.push(`a eventually generates all primes of ${n}; S hits 0`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}A = 0`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`primes of ${n} dividing ${a}: ${factorizeInt(n).filter(([p])=>a%p===0).map(f=>f[0]).join(', ') || 'none'}`);
          lines.push(`"killed" factor d = ${d}`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}(ℤ/${n}) ≅ ℤ/${m}`);
          if(m>1 && factorizeInt(m).length===1 && factorizeInt(m)[0][1]===1){
            lines.push(`local? yes — residue field 𝔽${m}`);
          }
        }
      } else {
        const p = Math.abs(elt);
        // check p is prime and divides n
        if(!isPrime(p) || n%p!==0){
          lines.push(`(${p}) is not a prime ideal of ℤ/${n} — use a prime dividing ${n}`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`𝔭 = (${p})`);
          lines.push(`S = ℤ/${n} − (${p}) = units ∪ (elements whose gcd with ${n} doesn't include ${p})`);
          // (Z/n)_𝔭 ≅ Z/p^k where p^k || n
          let k = 0, N = n; while(N%p===0){ N/=p; k++; }
          const pk = Math.pow(p,k);

          lines.push(`local ring — unique maximal ideal (${p}), residue field 𝔽${p}`);
          lines.push(`this is the "stalk at the point (${p}) ∈ Spec(ℤ/${n})"`);
        }
      }
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  ringSel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  modeSel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  eltIn.addEventListener('input', compute);
  compute();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 3: localization =============================== */
(function(){
  const ringSel = $('#loc-ring'), modeSel = $('#loc-mode'),
        eltIn = $('#loc-elt'), out = $('#loc-out');

  function compute(){
    const ring = ringSel.value, mode = modeSel.value;
    let elt = parseInt(eltIn.value,10);
    if(!isFinite(elt)){ out.textContent='bad element'; return; }
    const lines = [];

    if(ring==='Z'){
      lines.push('A = ℤ');
      if(mode==='f'){
        if(elt===0){ lines.push('inverting 0 kills everything: ℤ[1/0] = 0'); out.textContent=lines.join('\n'); return; }
        lines.push(`S = {1, ${elt}, ${elt}², …}`);
        const fact = factorizeInt(Math.abs(elt));
        const pstr = fact.map(([p,e])=>e===1?`${p}`:`${p}^${e}`).join(' · ') || '1';
        lines.push(`S^{-1}ℤ = ℤ[1/${elt}]`);
        if(fact.length){
          lines.push(`        = ℤ[1/${fact.map(f=>f[0]).join(', 1/')}]`);
        }
        lines.push(`Spec = Spec(ℤ) − V(${elt}) = {(p) : p prime, p ∤ ${elt}}`);
        lines.push(`removed points: ${fact.map(f=>`(${f[0]})`).join(', ') || 'none'}`);
        lines.push('local? no (infinitely many primes remain)');
      } else {
        if(elt===0){
          lines.push('S = ℤ − (0) = all non-zero integers');
          lines.push('S^{-1}ℤ = ℚ (fraction field)');
          lines.push('this is ℤ localized at the generic point (0)');
          lines.push('local ring: maximal ideal (0), residue field ℚ');
        } else if(isPrime(Math.abs(elt))){
          const p = Math.abs(elt);
          lines.push(`𝔭 = (${p})`);
          lines.push(`S = ℤ − (${p}) = {integers coprime to ${p}}`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}ℤ = ℤ_{(${p})} = {a/b : p ∤ b}`);
          lines.push(`this is a DVR — local, maximal ideal (${p}), residue field 𝔽${p}`);
          lines.push(`Spec = {(0), (${p})} — the generic point and the closed point`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`(${elt}) is not prime in ℤ — pick a prime or 0`);
        }
      }
    } else {
      // Z/n
      const n = ring==='Z12'?12: ring==='Z30'?30: 6;
      lines.push(`A = ℤ/${n}`);
      if(mode==='f'){
        const a = mod(elt, n);
        const g = gcd(a, n);
        lines.push(`invert ${a} ∈ ℤ/${n}`);
        lines.push(`S = {1, ${a}, ${a}², …} mod ${n}`);
        // In Z/n, inverting a: a has finite powers, eventually periodic. S^{-1}A = A/(ann) essentially.
        // S^{-1}(Z/n) = Z/(n/gcd(n,a^∞)) where gcd(n,a^∞) = radical stuff.
        // Actually: inverting a in Z/n gives Z/n[1/a]. Let d = gcd(n, a^k) for large k.
        // Result: Z/n localized at {powers of a} = Z/(n/d), where d = largest divisor of n whose prime factors divide a.
        let d = 1;
        // primes of n dividing a:
        for(const [p] of factorizeInt(n)){
          if(a % p === 0){
            // find largest power of p dividing n
            let q = 1; while(n % (q*p) === 0) q *= p;
            d *= q;
          }
        }
        const m = n/d;
        if(m===1){
          lines.push(`a eventually generates all primes of ${n}; S hits 0`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}A = 0`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`primes of ${n} dividing ${a}: ${factorizeInt(n).filter(([p])=>a%p===0).map(f=>f[0]).join(', ') || 'none'}`);
          lines.push(`"killed" factor d = ${d}`);
          lines.push(`S^{-1}(ℤ/${n}) ≅ ℤ/${m}`);
          if(m>1 && factorizeInt(m).length===1 && factorizeInt(m)[0][1]===1){
            lines.push(`local? yes — residue field 𝔽${m}`);
          }
        }
      } else {
        const p = Math.abs(elt);
        // check p is prime and divides n
        if(!isPrime(p) || n%p!==0){
          lines.push(`(${p}) is not a prime ideal of ℤ/${n} — use a prime dividing ${n}`);
        } else {
          lines.push(`𝔭 = (${p})`);
          lines.push(`S = ℤ/${n} − (${p}) = units ∪ (elements whose gcd with ${n} doesn't include ${p})`);
          // (Z/n)_𝔭 ≅ Z/p^k where p^k || n
          let k = 0, N = n; while(N%p===0){ N/=p; k++; }
          const pk = Math.pow(p,k);
          lines.push(`(ℤ/${n})_{(${p})} ≅ ℤ/${pk}`);
          lines.push(`local ring — unique maximal ideal (${p}), residue field 𝔽${p}`);
          lines.push(`this is the "stalk at the point (${p}) ∈ Spec(ℤ/${n})"`);
        }
      }
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }

  ringSel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  modeSel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  eltIn.addEventListener('input', compute);
  compute();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
9. Flatness

Tensoring is right-exact but not always left-exact. An $A$-module $M$ is flat if tensoring with $M$ preserves injections — equivalently, preserves all short exact sequences:

$0\to N'\to N\to N''\to 0$ remains exact after $-\otimes_A M$.

Equivalently: $\mathrm{Tor}_1^A(M,-)=0$. The implication chain is

free $\Rightarrow$ projective $\Rightarrow$ flat $\Rightarrow$ torsion-free,

and over a PID the last implication reverses, so over $\mathbb{Z}$: flat = torsion-free. Hence $\mathbb{Z}/n$ is not flat over $\mathbb{Z}$ for any $n\ge 2$.

The canonical failure. The sequence $0\to\mathbb{Z}\xrightarrow{n}\mathbb{Z}\to\mathbb{Z}/n\to 0$ tensored with $\mathbb{Z}/n$ gives $$\mathbb{Z}/n\xrightarrow{n=0}\mathbb{Z}/n\to\mathbb{Z}/n\to 0,$$ so the first map becomes zero and is no longer injective. The kernel is $\mathrm{Tor}_1^\mathbb{Z}(\mathbb{Z}/n,\mathbb{Z}/n)=\mathbb{Z}/n$.

params.bodyScript:
<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 4: flatness =============================== */
(function(){
  // Express each group as its order (0 = Z)
  function parse(s){
    if(s==='Z') return 0;
    if(s==='Q') return -1;            // special: Q
    if(s==='Z[1/2]') return -2;       // special: Z[1/2]
    const m = s.match(/^Z\/(\d+)$/);
    if(m) return parseInt(m[1],10);
    return null;
  }
  function fmt(x){
    if(x===0) return 'ℤ';
    if(x===-1) return 'ℚ';
    if(x===-2) return 'ℤ[1/2]';
    if(x===1) return '0';
    return `ℤ/${x}`;
  }
  // Tensor over Z of two such objects
  function tens(a,b){
    // handle Q, Z[1/2]
    if(a===-1 || b===-1){
      // ⊗ Q: kills torsion and tensors ranks
      if(a===-1 && b===-1) return -1;
      const other = a===-1 ? b : a;
      if(other===0) return -1;     // Z ⊗ Q = Q
      if(other>0) return 1;        // torsion ⊗ Q = 0
    }
    if(a===-2 || b===-2){
      // ⊗ Z[1/2]: kills 2-torsion; keeps odd torsion
      if(a===-2 && b===-2) return -2;
      const other = a===-2 ? b : a;
      if(other===0) return -2;
      if(other>0){
        // Z/n ⊗ Z[1/2] = Z[1/2]/n = (Z/n)[1/2], which kills 2-power part of n
        let odd = other; while(odd%2===0) odd/=2;
        return odd===1 ? 1 : odd;
        // technically this is (Z/odd)[1/2]=Z/odd, since 2 is a unit mod odd
      }
    }
    if(a===0 && b===0) return 0;
    if(a===0) return b;
    if(b===0) return a;
    if(a===1 || b===1) return 1;
    return gcd(a,b);
  }
  function mulBy(n, M){
    // "multiplication by n" map: M -> M (as an abelian group). Return kernel and image as order values.
    // For M of order m>0: ker(·n on Z/m) = Z/gcd(n,m); image = n·(Z/m) has order m/gcd(n,m), which as subgroup ≅ Z/(m/gcd(n,m))
    // For M=Z: ker = 0; image = nZ
    // For M=Q: ker = 0; image = Q
    // For M=Z[1/2] with n>0: ker=0 unless n has odd factor? Actually multiplication by n on Z[1/2] has kernel 0 always (torsion-free)
    if(M===0){ return { ker: 1, coker: n===1?1:n, injective: true }; }
    if(M===-1){ return { ker:1, coker:1, injective:true }; } // Q torsion-free divisible
    if(M===-2){
      let odd = n; while(odd%2===0) odd/=2;
      return { ker:1, coker: odd===1?1:odd, injective:true };
    }
    if(M===1){ return {ker:1, coker:1, injective:true}; }
    const g = gcd(n, M);
    // kernel = Z/g; coker = Z/g? Actually:
    // on Z/m, multiplication by n has kernel = Z/gcd(n,m); image has order m/gcd(n,m); coker has order gcd(n,m).
    return { ker: g===1?1:g, coker: g===1?1:g, injective: g===1 };
  }

  const elA = $('#fl-a'), elB = $('#fl-b'), elN = $('#fl-n'), elM = $('#fl-m'),
        go = $('#fl-go'), out = $('#fl-out');

  function compute(){
    const A = parse(elA.value), B = parse(elB.value), M = parse(elM.value);
    const n = Math.max(1, parseInt(elN.value,10)||1);
    if(A===null||B===null||M===null){ out.textContent='bad input'; return; }
    // Is 0 -> A -n-> B -> C -> 0 actually exact? Need: multiplication by n on A -> B is injective, and quotient is C.
    // For simplicity: require A and B such that ·n: A -> B is well-defined and injective.
    // A -> B is injective iff multiplication by n kills nobody in A AND image has order |A| (no collapse).
    // Easiest: just check |A|·n ≤ |B| or similar. Here we just compute assuming user provides a valid SES.
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(`SES:  0 → ${fmt(A)} —·${n}→ ${fmt(B)} → C → 0`);
    // compute C = B / (n·A in B)
    let C;
    if(B===0){
      if(A===0) C = n===1?1:n;
      else if(A>0) C = 1; // injection of Z/k into Z impossible unless k=1
      else C = 0; // Q into Z impossible
    } else if(B>0){
      if(A===0){
        // image of n·Z in Z/B is nZ/B = Z/gcd(n,B) if n and B... actually image = n·(Z mod B). subgroup of order B/gcd(n,B), quotient of order gcd(n,B)
        C = gcd(n,B)===1?1:gcd(n,B);
      } else {
        // image of n·(Z/A) in Z/B: makes sense if A | B / gcd(n,B) or something. treat as ker(Z/B -> Z/(B - nA)), for user-selected A>0 we just compute formally as Z/(B / (n·A))
        // we'll assume valid: C has order B/A (for Z/A -> Z/B via nA=B... hack)
        // Actually: embed Z/A as the subgroup (B/A)Z/B provided A | B; in that case ·n just describes which subgroup.
        if(B%A===0){
          C = A===1?1:(B/A);
        } else C = null;
      }
    }
    if(C===null){
      lines.push('(this choice of A, B does not form a valid SES — pick compatible sizes)');
      out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
      return;
    }
    lines.push(`        (so C ≅ ${fmt(C)})`);
    lines.push('');
    // now tensor with M
    const tA = tens(A,M), tB = tens(B,M), tC = tens(C,M);
    lines.push(`tensor with M = ${fmt(M)}:`);
    lines.push(`      ${fmt(tA)} —·${n}→ ${fmt(tB)} → ${fmt(tC)} → 0`);
    const mb = mulBy(n, tA);
    const inj = mb.injective;
    const ker = mb.ker;
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`ker(·${n} on ${fmt(tA)}) = ${fmt(ker)}`);
    if(inj){
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('exactness preserved at the left end.');
      if(M===0 || M===-1){
        lines.push(`(${fmt(M)} is flat — free / torsion-free over ℤ)`);
      } else if(M===-2){
        lines.push('ℤ[1/2] is flat (a localization of ℤ).');
      }
    } else {
      lines.push('');
      lines.push('INJECTIVITY FAILS — multiplication by '+n+' has kernel '+fmt(ker));
      lines.push(`so 0 → ${fmt(tA)} → ${fmt(tB)} is not exact on the left.`);
      lines.push(`Tor₁^ℤ(M, C) ≅ ${fmt(ker)} (this is the obstruction)`);
      lines.push(`→ ${fmt(M)} is NOT flat over ℤ.`);
    }
    out.textContent = lines.join('\n');
  }
  go.addEventListener('click', compute);
  [elA,elB,elN,elM].forEach(e=>e.addEventListener('change', compute));
  elN.addEventListener('input', compute);
  compute();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
Recipe: tensoring with $M$ is a functor, so it turns $0\to A\xrightarrow{n}B\to C\to 0$ into $A\otimes M\xrightarrow{n}B\otimes M\to C\otimes M\to 0$ (the tail is exact; the head might not be). Injectivity of "multiplication by $n$" on $A\otimes M$ is what flatness of $M$ buys you. Whenever $M$ has $n$-torsion, you lose it.

Two facts organize the rest of the theory. Local criterion. For $A$ Noetherian and $M$ finitely generated, $M$ is flat iff $M_\mathfrak{p}$ is free over $A_\mathfrak{p}$ for every prime $\mathfrak{p}$; equivalently $\mathrm{Tor}_1^A(A/\mathfrak{m},M)=0$ at every maximal ideal. Flat = geometric fibres nice. A morphism $f\colon X\to Y$ is flat iff $\mathcal{O}_{X,x}$ is flat over $\mathcal{O}_{Y,f(x)}$ everywhere; in a flat family the Hilbert polynomial of the fibre is locally constant, which is the right notion of "continuously varying scheme".

See also
  • Chain complexes and homology
  • Exact sequences

blocks.html:
10. Nakayama's lemma

Let $(A,\mathfrak{m})$ be a local ring (one maximal ideal) with residue field $k=A/\mathfrak{m}$.

Nakayama. If $M$ is a finitely generated $A$-module with $\mathfrak{m}M=M$, then $M=0$.

The standard use: $M/\mathfrak{m}M$ is a $k$-vector space, and a family $m_1,\dots,m_r\in M$ generates $M$ iff their images generate $M/\mathfrak{m}M$. So the dimension of $M/\mathfrak{m}M$ is the minimal number of generators.

Applied to $M=\mathfrak{m}$ itself: $\mathfrak{m}/\mathfrak{m}^2$ is a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space whose dual is the Zariski tangent space at the closed point. For a regular local ring this dimension equals $\dim A$; the discrepancy $\dim_k(\mathfrak{m}/\mathfrak{m}^2) - \dim A$ measures singularity.

For $A = k[x_1,\dots,x_n]_{(x_1-a_1,\dots,x_n-a_n)}/(f)$ (the local ring of $V(f)$ at a point $p=(a_1,\dots,a_n)$), the Zariski tangent space is simply the kernel of the Jacobian of $f$ evaluated at $p$:

$T_pV(f) = \ker\left(\left.\nabla f\right|_p\colon k^n\to k\right).$

If $V(f)$ has dimension $n-1$ and $T_p$ has dimension $n-1$ also, $p$ is smooth. If $T_p = k^n$ — i.e. all partials vanish — $p$ is singular.
